info.title: yuniql
info.description:
Copyright 2019 (C) Rodel E. Dagumampan
check        Check and test connectivity to target database server.
init         Initializes target directory with required directory structure.
verify       Runs migration like the run command but all changes will not be
committed.
run          Runs migration up to latest available version or up to specific
version.
apply        Alias to run command. Use this when yuniql run creates conflict
such as when used in docker run.
list         Shows all the migrations applied to target database.
vnext        Increments to next version.
erase        Discovers all scripts in the _erase directory and executes them
in single transaction.
destroy      Drops previously deployed database.
platforms    Shows suported platforms infromation and sample usage.
baseline     Scripts selected database objects to form your v0.00 schema.
[Future development]
rebase       Consolidates all existing versions, archive them and create a new
v0.00 baseline. [Future development]
help         Display more information on a specific command.
version      Display version information.

commands.name: check
commands.description: Check and test connectivity to target database server.

options.name: --platform
options.description: Target database platform. Default is sqlserver.

options.name: --plugins-path
options.description:
The location of plugins. The default location is
current location of the yuniql assemblies.

options.name: --connection-string
options.description:
Connection string to target database server
instance.

options.name: --command-timeout
options.description:
(Default: 30) The time in seconds to wait for the
command to execute.

options.name: --path
options.description: Path to initialize.

options.name: --debug
options.description: Print debug information including all raw scripts.

options.name: --trace-sensitive-data
options.description:
(Default: false) Include sensitive data like
connection string in the log messages.

options.name: --trace-to-file
options.description:
(Default: false) Trace logs are also written on
file in addition to console output.

options.name: --trace-to-directory
options.description:
Trace logs files are placed in specific directory
using --trace-directory parameter.

options.name: --trace-directory
options.description: Directory path where the log files will be created.

options.name: --help
options.description: Display this help screen.

options.name: --version
options.description: Display version information.

commands.name: verify
commands.description: Runs migration like the run command but all changes will not be

options.name: --target-version
options.description:
Target version to migrate into and skipping
versions greater.

options.name: --auto-create-db
options.description: (Default: false) Create database automatically.

options.name: --token
options.description: Replace tokens using the passed key-value pairs.

options.name: --bulk-separator
options.description: (Default: ,) Bulk import file values separator.

options.name: --bulk-batch-size
options.description:
(Default: 0) Bulk import batch size for platforms
supporting batch rows.

options.name: --environment
options.description: Environment code for environment-aware scripts.

options.name: --meta-schema
options.description: Schema name for schema versions table.

options.name: --meta-table
options.description: Table name for schema versions table.

options.name: --transaction-mode
options.description:
(Default: session) Transaction mode to use in the
migration.

options.name: --continue-after-failure
options.description:
(Default: false) Skip failed script and continue
with migration (Only for platforms which doesn't
fully support transactions).

options.name: --require-cleared-draft
options.description:
(Default: false) When set, migration will fail if
the _draft directory is not empty. This option
ideal when targeting staging/production
environment.
